clinical_coherence.notes: 78F East Asian with AF (self-discontinued warfarin 3 weeks ago due to nosebleeds), HTN, DM2, HFpEF, DVT history. Presenting with right hemiplegia and nonfluent aphasia. Vitals appropriate. Clinical picture coherent. BMI discrepancy: demographics say 25.2 but PMH lists 'Obesity (BMI 31.2)' -- this is an internal inconsistency.

tool_output_consistency.notes: MRI, ECG, labs, CTA, echo all concordant. Left MCA territory infarction with M1 occlusion. AF confirmed. INR 1.1. HFpEF with dilated LA on echo. All consistent with cardioembolic mechanism.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD I63.9 non-specific. Critical actions comprehensive. Appropriate note about warfarin side effects (epistaxis) leading to non-adherence and recommendation for DOAC transition. Contraindicated actions include restarting warfarin given intolerance.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Appropriately straightforward. Very similar structure to S01-S03. The core diagnostic path is clear.

demographic_plausibility.notes: BMI contradiction: demographics list 25.2 but PMH says 'Obesity (BMI 31.2)'. This is a data integrity issue. Otherwise, 78F East Asian with AF, HTN, DM2 is plausible. The PMH pattern is again very similar to S02/S03 (same medications: amlodipine, losartan, metformin, glipizide, rosuvastatin).

educational_value.notes: Standard stroke case. Limited novelty beyond S01-S03. DVT history adds minor clinical texture. The epistaxis-driven warfarin discontinuation is a realistic scenario but covered similarly in S02.

issues_found: BMI discrepancy: demographics say 25.2 but PMH says obesity with BMI 31.2
Nearly identical PMH/medication template to S02 and S03
